articles.title: Lynn mother, educator avoids deportation for now
articles.description:
The mother from Guatemala was put on ICE's radar in 2013 during a traffic stop in which her attorney said she was racially profiled by a security worker and later arrested.

The post Lynn mother, educator avoids deportation for now appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lynn mother and educator from Guatemala avoids deportation after ICE involvement since 2013.
Traffic stop in 2013 led to her being racially profiled and arrested, according to her attorney.
Her case highlights ongoing immigration challenges affecting families in the Boston area.

articles.title: Night Shift’s outdoor beer gardens are back for the 2026 season. Here’s what to know.
articles.description:
The brewery’s seasonal beer gardens at the Esplanade, in Herter Park, and Dewey Square are reopening with new beers, food trucks, and dog-friendly seating.

The post Night Shift’s outdoor beer gardens are back for the 2026 season. Here’s what to know. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Night Shift's outdoor beer gardens reopen for the 2026 season on May 12.
Locations include Esplanade, Herter Park, and Dewey Square, featuring new beers and food trucks.
Dog-friendly seating enhances community engagement for Boston residents this summer.

articles.title: Sail Boston 250 is coming up. Here’s where to learn to sail.
articles.description:
Witness over 25 countries’ tall ships as they sail into Boston Harbor by learning how to sail yourself.

The post Sail Boston 250 is coming up. Here’s where to learn to sail. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Sail Boston 250 will showcase over 25 countries' tall ships in Boston Harbor.
Residents can learn to sail in preparation for the event starting May 12, 2026.
The event promotes local tourism and community engagement in Boston's maritime culture.

articles.title: Suspected Memorial Drive gunman’s criminal record comes under scrutiny after Cambridge shooting
articles.description:
At the time of Monday’s shooting, Tyler Brown was on probation for firing several rounds at police officers in the South End in May 2020.

The post Suspected Memorial Drive gunman’s criminal record comes under scrutiny after Cambridge shooting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tyler Brown, 29, was arrested for a shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge.
Brown was on probation for a May 2020 incident involving gunfire at police.
Residents express concern over public safety due to Brown's criminal history.

articles.title: Get one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest for a limited time
articles.description:
The sale ends May 14.

The post Get one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest for a limited time appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Southwest Airlines offers one-way flights from Boston starting at $59.
The limited-time sale ends on May 14, 2026.
Boston residents can save significantly on travel during this promotional period.

articles.title: Parts of Storrow Drive, Soldiers Field Road to face nightly closures
articles.description:
The shutdown, scheduled from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m. each night, will continue throughout August.

The post Parts of Storrow Drive, Soldiers Field Road to face nightly closures appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will close nightly from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m.
Closures will continue throughout August, affecting local traffic patterns.
Boston residents should plan alternative routes to avoid delays during construction.

articles.title: Self-serve ticket machine yields $10 million winner at Boston supermarket
articles.description:
The ticket was purchased at a Stop & Shop in Dorchester.

The post Self-serve ticket machine yields $10 million winner at Boston supermarket appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Self-serve ticket machine at Stop & Shop in Dorchester sold a $10 million winning ticket.
The winning ticket was purchased recently, boosting local excitement and potential spending.
This win may encourage more residents to play the lottery, impacting local businesses.

articles.title: Equestrian retailer Dover Saddlery warns of more than 100 potential layoffs
articles.description:
The Littleton-based company may close if it doesn’t sell or get an infusion of funds.

The post Equestrian retailer Dover Saddlery warns of more than 100 potential layoffs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Dover Saddlery in Littleton warns of over 100 potential layoffs.
The company may close without a sale or funding infusion.
Local economy could suffer significant job losses if layoffs occur.

articles.title: Officials: Newly opened Mass Pike ramp will decrease traffic congestion
articles.description:
Previously, motorists accessed all I-495 exits from a single point, causing increased crash rates and traffic, MassDOT says.

The post Officials: Newly opened Mass Pike ramp will decrease traffic congestion appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MassDOT opened a new Mass Pike ramp to improve traffic flow.
Previously, all I-495 exits were accessed from a single point, increasing crash rates.
The new ramp aims to significantly reduce congestion for Boston commuters.
